title: viola alba subsp. cretica
intro: Viola alba subsp. cretica, also known as the Cretan white violet, is a subspecies of the common violet plant that is native to the island of Crete. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Viola alba subsp. cretica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat conditions like respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations.
